primary_factors: No accumulating snow/precip expected (0"), so no plowing-capacity driver for closure
Primary hazard is dense/freezing fog during bus window (roughly 5:30–8:30 AM) with potential near-zero visibility and patchy bridge/overpass glaze
Road network expected generally passable on trunklines/arterials; risk concentrated on rural low-lying routes, bridges, subdivision streets, and parking lots/sidewalks
No confirmed domino effect: closings pages show no active closures/delays; no official district announcement found
Power-outage wildcard exists (Consumers reporting ~44k out systemwide), but Rockford/Kent-specific facility impacts are unconfirmed and trend is decreasing
decision_rationale: All expert inputs converge that Thu 2026-03-19 is not a snowfall or extreme-cold closure setup for Rockford: forecast snowfall is 0" and verified wind chills are far above the ≤-20°F automatic-closure thresholds. The only credible pathway to a closure is operational safety from freezing fog (reduced sight distance for buses, kids at stops, and stop-arm events) combined with spot icing on bridges/overpasses and untreated local streets/parking lots. Historically, Michigan districts more often mitigate this exact scenario with a short delay rather than an all-day closure, unless there is widespread sustained ≤1/4-mile visibility AND confirmed glazing/black-ice problems across multiple routes. News intel shows no active closings/delays postings yet and no neighboring-district domino signal. Power outages are a secondary wildcard (systemwide outages remain elevated), but there is no Rockford-specific confirmation; outage totals are also trending downward. Given Michigan’s high bar for full cancellations and the lack of confirming closure signals, the most Brier-optimal call is a low (but non-zero) all-day closure probability, with a higher conditional risk of a 1–2 hour delay if fog verifies as widespread at 5:30–6:30 AM.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 6.0% spread. Average probability: 8%.
